tool.name: KAT Cold

regex.description: KAT C++ heap corruption — output files were still produced correctly

tool.command:
#if $assembly.ext == 'fasta.gz': zcat '$assembly' > 'assembly.fasta' && #else: cp '$assembly' 'assembly.fasta' && #end if
## Link read input files with appropriate extensions #for $i, $f in enumerate($input_files): @SET_EXT@ cp '$f' 'input${i}${ext}' && #end for
kat cold -o kat-cold -t "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" -x '$matrix_options.gc_bins' -y '$matrix_options.cvg_bins' -m '$kmer_options.mer_len' -H '$kmer_options.hash_size' #if str($kmer_options.five_prime_trim).strip(): --5ptrim '$kmer_options.five_prime_trim' #end if -p '$output_options.output_type' 'assembly.fasta' #for $i, $f in enumerate($input_files): @SET_EXT@ 'input${i}${ext}' #end for

param.name: input_files
param.label: Reads
param.help: One or more FASTA/FASTQ read files (gzip accepted) or a single pre-counted Jellyfish hash file.

param.label: K-mer length
param.help: Larger values provide more discriminating power between k-mers but require more memory and result in lower coverage. (default: 27)

param.label: Hash size
param.help: Hash size for k-mer counting of the reads. The assembly uses half this value. Doubled automatically if too small, increasing runtime and memory usage. (default: 100000000)

param.label: GC bins
param.help: Number of bins for the GC data when creating the contamination matrix. (default: 1001)

param.label: Coverage bins
param.help: Number of bins for the coverage data when creating the contamination matrix. (default: 1001)

has_text.text: gi|556503834|ref|NC_000913.3| Escherichia coli str. K-12 substr. MG1655, complete genome
gi|556503834|ref|NC_000913.3| Escherichia coli str. K-12 substr. MG1655, complete genome

tool.help:
**KAT COLD - Contig Length and Duplication Analysis**
KAT (K-mer Analysis Toolkit) COLD calculates median read k-mer coverage, assembly k-mer coverage, and GC% across each sequence in a provided assembly.
-----
**What it does**
For each contig or scaffold in the assembly, COLD calculates:
- **Median read k-mer coverage** - how well reads cover the sequence - **Assembly k-mer coverage** - coverage as seen in the assembly itself - **GC%** - GC content of the sequence
Results are presented as a tabular stats file and a scatter plot where:
- Each point represents one sequence - The **y-axis** shows median read k-mer coverage - The **x-axis** shows GC% - Points are **colored** similarly to spectra-cn plots (indicating duplication level) - Points are **sized** according to sequence length
This allows rapid identification of contaminant sequences (outlier GC%), collapsed repeats (unusually high coverage), and other assembly artefacts.
-----
**Inputs**
- **Assembly**: A FASTA file of the genome assembly (gzip accepted). - **Reads**: One or more FASTA/FASTQ read files (gzip compression accepted), or a single pre-counted Jellyfish hash file.
-----
**Outputs**
- **Per-sequence stats** (`.stats`): Tab-separated table with per-sequence statistics including sequence name, length, GC%, and median k-mer coverage. - **Contamination matrix** (`.contamination.mx`): 2D matrix of GC% vs coverage bins, used to identify contamination. - **Scatter plot**: Visual plot in the selected format (PNG, PDF, or PostScript) with each contig plotted by GC% and coverage.
-----
**Notes**
- Increase the **k-mer length** for more discriminating power at the cost of higher memory usage and lower effective coverage. - Use **Disable automatic hash growing** when working with very large genomes or under strict memory limits to prevent unexpected memory spikes. - The default **hash size** (100 million) suits most datasets; adjust upward for large or high-coverage genomes. - For multiple read files, **5' trim** values can be specified per-file using comma-separated integers (e.g. `5,10,0`).
